adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (ESP -1000 / draw +725 / KSA +1450 -> de-vig ~83/11/6). Spain were held 0-0 by Cape Verde in their opener and sit bottom of a level Group H, but de la Fuente welcomes Nico Williams and Lamine Yamal back into the XI, which should unlock the attack; Spain have beaten Saudi Arabia in all three previous meetings (9-2 agg) and KSA have lost nine straight WC games against UEFA sides. A bounce-back 2-0 Spain win is the central case; the model's 97% is a hair high but the market agrees Spain are overwhelming.

factors.note: Model 97% vs market 83%; anchored to market
Nico Williams & Lamine Yamal back in XI after the goalless opener -> sharper attack
0-3 all-time vs Spain (9-2 agg); 9 straight WC losses to UEFA teams

adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (BEL -230 / draw +360 / IRN +650; Kalshi 68/21/12 -> ~67/21/13). Both drew their openers (Belgium 1-1 with Egypt, Iran 2-2 with New Zealand) in a level Group G. adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (URU -200 / draw +300 / CPV +600 -> de-vig ~63/24/13), with the draw a touch elevated. Uruguay (Bielsa; Valverde, Nunez) are clear favourites and the class side, but Cape Verde just frustrated Spain to a 0-0 with a disciplined five-man low block and seven Vozinha saves -- the same approach that could keep this tight (Under 2.5 is favoured). A low-scoring 1-0 Uruguay win is the central case; the model's 92% badly overstates how easily they break a deep block.

Two-time WC winner; Valverde & Nunez quality, but goals may come at a premium

adjusted.reasoning: Anchored to the market (EGY -167 / draw +300 / NZL +450 -> de-vig ~59/24/17). Both drew their openers in a level Group G, making this an effective six-pointer. Egypt are clear favourites: a flawless qualifying campaign (5W-1D, 9 scored 0 conceded), that defensive solidity carrying into the tournament, and Mohamed Salah (67 intl goals) the matchwinner against an ageing New Zealand back line. The model's 70% Egypt was a bit strong; market ~59% is the better read. A 0-1 Egypt win is the central case, though NZ are physical and the draw is live.

Salah threat; flawless qualifying defence (0 conceded), settled & solid
Six-pointer in a level group -> both motivated; NZ physical, draw well in play
